headline: Kelantan Well Water Contamination Affects 45,000 Households
context: SPAN's 2025 survey found 45,000 households in Kelantan rely on untreated well water, with 38% of tested wells exceeding safe limits for coliform bacteria or heavy metals.

cards.big: Kelantan has the lowest treated water coverage in Peninsular Malaysia at 82%.
cards.sub: 45,000 households depend on wells. 38% of those wells are contaminated.

cards.big: AKSB's treated water network has not expanded since 2018.
cards.sub: Kelantan rejected federal takeover of water services, maintaining state control with limited capital.

cards.big: Gastrointestinal illness rates in well-dependent areas are 3.4x the national average.
cards.sub: Healthcare costs for preventable waterborne disease in Kelantan exceeded RM28M in 2024.

cards.big: Federal offer: RM1.2B for water infrastructure if Kelantan restructures AKSB.
cards.sub: Kelantan rejected the condition. The money remains unspent. The wells remain contaminated.

cards.big: 45,000 families drink unsafe water because two governments cannot agree on who controls the pipes.
Clean water should not be contingent on political negotiations. The federal offer must be restructured to separate infrastructure investment from governance reform.
